title: barringtonia longifolia
intro: Barringtonia longifolia, also known as the "Putat" or "Sea Poison Tree", is a plant species native to the coastal regions of the Indo-Pacific area, characterized by its beautiful white flowers and edible fruits. The medicinal properties of Barringtonia longifolia include its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and provide relief from skin irritations and wounds. Traditional herbal preparations of Barringtonia longifolia include infusions, decoctions, and topical applications, where the leaves and bark are often used to create medicinal remedies.
